items.body: The Memorial Tournament returns to Jack Nicklaus’s Muirfield Village in Dublin, Ohio, for its 50th edition and the penultimate Signature Event of the season. Scottie Scheffler heads the field as a two-time defending champion and betting favorite, chasing a three-peat that only Tiger Woods has matched at this venue. Rory McIlroy, Cameron Young, and Xander Schauffele lead the challengers in a star-packed, limited field, with Muirfield’s demanding Nicklaus layout expected to reward precision, course management, and steady ball striking over the final major tune-up before the U.S. Open.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+265 to +300):
items.body: The two-time defending champion and clear betting favorite, with a dominant track record at Muirfield and the best all-around form in the field.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+870 to +1000):
items.body: The highest-profile challenger in the market, returning after the PGA Championship with the firepower to contend on a course that suits elite ball strikers.

items.label: Cameron Young (+1275 to +1600):
items.body: A rising contender whose length and improving iron play make him a live option if he keeps the driver in play on a demanding test.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1700 to +1800):
items.body: A polished, consistent performer whose tee-to-green game fits Muirfield well when his putter cooperates.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+2000):
items.body: An elite iron player and proven winner whose accuracy and patience are tailor-made for one of the Tour’s toughest setups.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+2000):
items.body: One of the season’s best ball strikers and a strong value play if he converts his steady top finishes into a breakthrough win.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2600):
items.body: The reigning FedExCup champion and a polished all-around player who can hang around on a course that rarely yields soft winning scores.

items.label: Russell Henley (+2700):
items.body: Coming off a playoff win at Colonial, his precision and iron play are a natural fit for Muirfield’s narrow corridors and demanding greens.

items.label: Ben Griffin (+3000 to +4600):
items.body: Last year’s runner-up to Scheffler and a live option if he recaptures the form that had him in the mix on Sunday in 2025.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: Muirfield Village Golf Club in Dublin, Ohio, a Jack Nicklaus design outside Columbus.

items.label: Established:
items.body: 1976, founded and hosted by Jack Nicklaus; the 2026 edition marks the tournament’s 50th anniversary.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Scottie Scheffler won in 2024 and 2025, joining Tiger Woods as the only back-to-back winners in event history.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: A Signature Event and one of the Tour’s most prestigious stops, honoring golf legends while raising millions for Greater Columbus charities.

items.label: Course:
items.body: Muirfield Village Golf Club, Dublin, Ohio.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: June 4 through June 7, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $20 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 72-player Signature Event, 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A demanding 7,569-yard par-72 Nicklaus layout that ranks among the Tour’s sternest tests and rewards fairways, greens, and disciplined course management.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Thursday and Friday on Golf Channel (2–6 p.m. ET); weekend rounds on Golf Channel (12:30–2:30 p.m. ET) and CBS (2:30–6 p.m. ET Saturday, 2:30–6:30 p.m. ET Sunday).

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs June 4 through June 7, 2026.
